prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response:
Hey there! 

Listen up for just a minute. You've been staring at that task all morning, haven't you? It's been looming over you like a storm cloud, weighing you down. But guess what? Storm clouds pass, and so will this feeling.

This task isn't a mountain - it's just a hill you haven't climbed yet. Every expert was once a beginner. Every marathon runner was once someone who could barely run a mile. You're about to join their club.

Take a deep breath. Right now. In... and out. That's it. The hard part is actually starting, and you've already done that by asking for this pep talk.

You've got everything you need right here - your brain, your hands, and your determination. There's no better time than right now to begin. Five minutes. That's all you need to commit to. Just five minutes of focus, and the momentum will build from there.

Don't worry about being perfect. Just worry about being productive. One step at a time. You can do this. Really, you can.

Now go. Start. The relief you'll feel when you begin will be better than the relief you'd feel by putting it off any longer. Go on - start now.
